title: Strengthening the Quad Act
plain_summary: This bill tells the Secretary of State to make a plan for working more closely with three other countries - Australia, India, and Japan. These four countries together are called the Quad. The bill also asks the Secretary of State to work with these countries to create a special group where members of Congress can meet regularly with lawmakers from the other three countries. The plan must explain how these countries can work together better on things like the economy, technology, and energy.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Strengthening the Quad Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Sense of Congress
sections.text: Congress believes that the United States should work closely with Australia, India, and Japan. These four countries are often called the Quadrilateral Dialogue or the Quad. The United States should strengthen this partnership to meet challenges in the Pacific region and promote a free and open Indo-Pacific area. This area should respect democratic values, follow the rule of law, and have market-driven economic growth without unfair influence or pressure from other countries. The United States should expand talks and cooperation through the Quad with other partners to support peace, prosperity, rule of law, freedom of navigation and flying over international waters, peaceful solutions to disagreements, and democratic strength in the Indo-Pacific. The first Quad leaders meeting on March twelfth, two thousand twenty one, created a foundation for important cooperation. The leaders promised to address shared challenges in public health, cyberspace, important technologies, fighting terrorism, quality infrastructure investment, and helping with disasters and emergencies in ocean areas. The Quad countries should work through organizations like the United States International Development Finance Corporation, the Japan International Cooperation Agency, and the Japan Bank for International Cooperation. They should also partner with development banks like the World Bank and the Asian Development Bank to fund development and infrastructure projects in the Indo-Pacific region that are competitive, transparent, and sustainable. President Biden made an important decision to elevate the Quad to the leader level to improve cooperation. All four countries should work to make sure the Quad Leaders Summit continues to happen regularly. The ambitious plan for ongoing cooperation created by the four leaders at the fifth Quad Leaders Summit in Hiroshima on May twentieth, two thousand twenty three, should continue. Secretary of State Marco Rubio hosted the Quad Foreign Ministers in Washington DC on January twenty first, two thousand twenty five, which was his first day in office. This showed the continued importance of cooperation between the four democracies. Creating a Quad Inter-Parliamentary Working Group will help maintain and deepen discussions between senior officials of the Quad countries on many different issues. This group will be modeled on successful long-standing bilateral inter-parliamentary groups between the United States and Mexico, Canada, and the United Kingdom, as well as other formal and informal parliamentary exchanges.

sections.title: Section 3  Strategy
sections.text: Within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, the Secretary of State must submit a strategy to the appropriate congressional committees for improving engagement and cooperation with the Quad. The strategy must include several things. First, it must describe how the United States plans to show democratic leadership in the Indo-Pacific through working with Australia, India, and Japan on shared interests and common challenges. Second, it must provide a summary of current and past Quad projects across the entire United States Government. These projects include promoting broad-based and inclusive economic growth and investment, advancing technology cooperation, energy innovation, climate mitigation and adaptation, physical and digital infrastructure development, education, disaster management, resilient supply chains including critical minerals, and global health security. The summary must also include proposals agreed to by all Quad countries since January two thousand twenty one to deepen existing security cooperation, intelligence sharing, economic partnerships, and multilateral coordination. It must also cover initiatives and agreements undertaken jointly with Quad countries and other like-minded partners in the Indo-Pacific on areas of shared interest since January two thousand twenty one. Third, the strategy must describe the diplomatic and bureaucratic barriers and obstacles to implementing and expanding existing streams of Quad cooperation. Fourth, it must provide a list of recommendations on how Congress could help address these barriers. This includes any new authorities needed to strengthen United States leadership in and contribution to existing and proposed Quad initiatives and programs, as well as additional resources needed to scale up and expand successful Quad initiatives and programs. In this section, appropriate congressional committees means the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate.

sections.title: Section 4  Establishment of Quad Inter-Parliamentary Working Group
sections.text: Within sixty days after this law is passed, the Secretary of State must try to start negotiations with the Governments of Australia, India, and Japan. The goal is to reach a written agreement to establish a Quad Inter-Parliamentary Working Group to help with closer cooperation on shared interests and values. When the governments of the Quad countries enter into a written agreement to establish a Quad Inter-Parliamentary Working Group, there will be a United States Group created. This United States Group will represent the United States at the Quad Inter-Parliamentary Working Group. The United States Group will have no more than twenty four Members of Congress. Half of these Members will be appointed by the Speaker of the House of Representatives, based on recommendations from the minority leader, from among Members of the House. At least four of these House members must be members of the Committee on Foreign Affairs. The other half will be appointed by the President Pro Tempore of the Senate, based on recommendations of the majority leader and minority leader of the Senate, from among Members of the Senate. At least four of these Senate members must be members of the Committee on Foreign Relations, unless the majority leader and minority leader decide otherwise. The United States Group will try to meet at least once a year with representatives and appropriate staff of the legislatures of Australia, India, and Japan, and any other country invited by mutual agreement of the Quad countries. These meetings can be held in the United States, in another Quad country during periods when Congress is not in session, or virtually. The Speaker of the House of Representatives will choose the chairperson or vice chairperson of the House delegation from among members of the Committee on Foreign Affairs. The President Pro Tempore of the Senate will choose the chairperson or vice chairperson of the Senate delegation from among members of the Committee on Foreign Relations. The United States Group may accept gifts or donations of services or property, subject to review and approval by the Committee on Ethics of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Ethics of the Senate. The certificate of the chairperson of the House or Senate delegation will be final and conclusive upon the accounting officers in auditing the accounts of the United States Group. The United States Group must submit a report to the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate at the end of each year. This report must outline the group's activities that year, including a description of its expenditures, and its recommendations for enhancing the Quad. Senior United States officials must provide regular updates and briefings to the United States Group, including before and after major Quadrilateral dialogues, to ensure close coordination with Congress.
